When you check his wheel description it says "Increase your Hand Limit by 2." The normal amount is 10 which means you can only have 10 cards at one time in your hand. With hand size wheels it increases it to 12 which lets you have more plays and options.

- Will be a while at the very least probably 6 months can check banner history here -> https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1gRDzdVHGfCC4qjt5aZYKuU9FWEfWdqREztNGeiczmRk/edit?gid=683020039#gid=683020039
- Game is pretty generous it throws standard pulls at you for just breathing and monthly silver income for F2P is about 6k silver (30 pulls guarantees 1 SSR for max pity) 
- Caveat being this is a dupe centric game but game does offset this by providing 1 universal dupe for both awakener and wheel monthly from shop

content: Pull income does not include permanent content though you get so many pulls both standard and limited by progressing story

Are there any other teams I should start working on? And what Enlightens should I be getting for them and what units? I dolphin'ed a little but I really only buy BPs and monthly. So I wanted to know which team should I focus on next and be smarter about characters I get and invest in. 

Currently I'm using 
- Twins/Winkle/Flex (Soon to be Horla hopefully, not a fan of Clem)
- GMurphy/Tulu/Faros/Goliath (Waiting for a Miryam solo rerun)
Who you will be replacing with miryam in gmurphy team
Depends, I guess? Faros offers more tentacle DMG amp, emergency shields, and card gathering.
But Goliath gives burstier turns?
Right? I'll probably replace Goliath until I'm more confident in playing GMurphy's team.

Holy stacked
- Main end game content is D-Tide where you need 5 unique teams (no repeated awakeners, wheels, and posses) You already have 3 solid units to build around for 3 teams: Pollux, Castor, and G!Murphy. It's just a matter of the other 2 teams.

- <:point:1356053112292511815> **Make sure you use Tawil, either your own or borrowed, in raid event to make sure you enjoy her playstyle**. With that decision Tawil becomes a usable DPS E1 with E2 being the ideal first breakpoint with the next and last one being OE. There are 2 mains teams you can build with her, Monowil or Bluewil
- Before you play Tawil make sure you have a hand size wheel for this team there's a lot of retain and drawing 
- Hameln is basically her forever partner lots of synergy. He does his job at E0 and happy to stay there but if you're wanting to do A5 D-Tide then E2 would be ideal.
- Bluewil is good for starting out. You have Caecus at E3 for this team for access to tentacles. Last slot being a flex spot some popular options being G!Ramona and Karen. This doc will explain the gameplay better than I can
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LpaiOrYJVfLmKmkbGEK88p3o987bWOO52P_70JuFdhc/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.xwadc55ryu90
- Monowil (Pure Chaos) is for very late game and requires a lot of investment. This team will want Ryker E1-2 as the other limited awakener and last slot being Karen. This team is faster but more frail than Bluewil. This team is a more popular version for A5, you could probably find replays in D-Tide leaderboard

- For last team you don't really have anyone I would recommend to build up or familiar with <:cry1:1356053046374961212> You could borrow a whale DPS to build around. Otherwise if you want your own unit then E3 Kath is a good investment as a strong and flexible DPS. If enjoy Aurita, Discard comp, or enjoy Corpo you can grab her to make a team. You have Tulu and Casiah for a full team. Corpo will ideally want E3 but you can stop at E2 and wait for monthly dupe. With this team you will want to buy Aurita dupes from shop. Note that the last rotations happen next week.

- After that will be wheels. You will eventually want all hand size wheels (Thais, Tawil, Castor, and G!Murphy). Your other wheel priorities will depend on your unit and wheel roster.

embeds.title: Bluewil guide
embeds.description: Bluewil Guide from: strawberry (This guide assumes you have E1+)  Introduction Bluewil = tawil + blue = choose from blue cards on exalt Tawil + battle = 20 cards/turn Tawil + Tawil wheel = happy Tawil E2 Tawil = happy Tawil Hameln = happy Tawil Please make her happy  The comp I will go over the...

Timeworn Ramona has a tendency to dramatically complicate your game plan with her exalt and tends to be more of a pain in the ass to use than regular Ramona if you're not making pumping posses all day your primary game plan, and relic reliance makes it hard to just point to a single thing and say "This is where you're fucking up" so you'd have to grab someone who's already very familiar with what's going on. It feels a little more time consuming than looking at a blue team and saying "Your Miryam isn't E3, so you can swap her out"

Like there are teams where you can basically describe your first three turns deterministically, like Helot Catena, and then there's shit like Tawil completely John fucking Maddening all over your board, and this team looks like it's in the upper half of that. 

Karen's skill ranks are low, but I don't know if that matters? Because she's just here to use Cuisine and have her retain cards sit in your hand so you draw other cards more often. Ryker's skill ranks are kinda low too, and that probably matters a great deal more. I have no idea why a guide written by Flares would suggest both hidden wheel and abyssal uterus.

A team centered around GMurphy requires her to deal damage, meaning you should grab more Enlightenments for her.
Within the options you have, GMurphy / Tulu (E1) / Miryam (E2) / Goliath is Arc 2 team (Arc 2/D-Tide/Events).
As for the Arc 1 team, GMurphy/Caecus/Murphy/Tulu is alright. I'm not sure about Caro/Aequor mix, thus being the reason I didn't place Thais in. If anything, you can try placing her instead of Tulu.

what foes goliath do in the gmurphy comp
i cant rly utilize him

The thing you have to be careful with GMurphy is tentacles stances cooldowns. That requires you to plan in advance, meaning that you have to consider the amount of tentacles for the burst turn and having Waltz cards to burst. You can use Waltz in passive stance to generate more tentacles, just plan ahead so that you will have Waltz again until your burst turn.  I don't think you will need to use Waltz in white stance for the shields since you have Murphy and Caecus on the team.
As for Thais, I'm not completely sure since I haven't played her.
Regarding the GMurphy stuff I mentioned above, I've only played her at E3, I don't remember what E1/E2 Enlightenments do, so the gameplay may vary.

He is a lemurian, so you get the buff for having a lemurian on GMurphy team (the Lemurian buff works only in Arc 2 ruleset). Within Arc 1, all he does is basically being a support for STR generation with his exalt + steal STR from enemies once he is E2.
so he just stays in act2
altho isnt most lemurian buffs 3 ppl max
feel like his str generation is insufficient unless he can use the steal?
Yea? Not including GMurphy, you can have 3 Lemurians on the team to get the max value buff.
Yeah, not much value from him within Arc 1, though there's still STR stealing and STR exalt generation.
what could be a good option then from what i have?
Yeah, unless there's a boss with str gen to steal from, his value is minimal. Again, it's only for Arc 1 ruleset, you can use him in Arc 2 just fine.
